# Parserino: Fast HTML5 Parser and DOM Manipulation for D

Parserino is a high-performance HTML5 parser and DOM manipulation library for the D programming language, built on the Lexbor engine.

## Core Types

### `Document`
The root of the DOM tree. Created from an HTML string.

- `this(string html)`: Constructor.
- `bool isValid`: True if the document is successfully parsed.
- `string title`: Property to get or set the content of the `<title>` tag.
- `Element head`: Returns the `<head>` element.
- `Element body`: Returns the `<body>` element.
- `Element createElement(string tagName)`: Creates a new element with the specified tag name.
- `Element createText(string text)`: Creates a text node.
- `Element createComment(string text)`: Creates a comment node (`<!-- text -->`).
- `Element fragment(string html)`: Creates a document fragment from a string.
- `string toString()`: Serializes the entire document back to HTML.
- `byId(string id)`: Finds an element by its ID (throws if not found).
- `byClass(string name)`: Returns a lazy range of elements with the specified class.
- `byTagName(string name)`: Returns a lazy range of elements with the specified tag name.
- `bySelector(string selector)`: Returns a lazy range of elements matching a CSS selector.
- `byComment(string comment)`: Returns a lazy range of comment nodes matching the specified text.

### `Element`
Represents a node in the DOM.

- `bool isValid`: True if the element is not null.
- `string name`: The tag name (e.g., "div", "p", "#text", "!--").
- `string id`: The `id` attribute value.
- `auto classes`: A range of classes (split by space).
- `auto attributes`: A range of `Attribute` structs (containing `name` and `value`).
- `bool hasAttribute(string attr)`: Checks for attribute existence.
- `string getAttribute(string attr)`: Retrieves an attribute's value.
- `void setAttribute(string name, string value)`: Sets or updates an attribute.
- `void removeAttribute(string attr)`: Removes an attribute.
- `string innerHTML`: Get/set the HTML content inside the element.
- `string innerText`: Get/set the text content (strips tags on get, escapes on set).
- `string outerHTML`: Get/set the HTML including the element itself.
- `void appendChild(T)(T el)`: Adds a child. `T` can be `Element`, `string` (added as text), or `FragmentString` (added as HTML).
- `void prependChild(T)(T el)`: Adds a child at the beginning.
- `void appendSibling(T)(T el)`: Adds a sibling after the current element.
- `void prependSibling(T)(T el)`: Adds a sibling before the current element.
- `bool remove()`: Detaches the element from its parent.
- `void replaceWith(T)(T el)`: Replaces the element with another.
- `void copyFrom(Element e, bool deep = true)`: Copies attributes and optionally children from another element.
- `Element parent`, `Element next`, `Element prev`: Navigation.
- `Element firstChild(bool includeAllElements = false)`: Returns the first child (optionally including text/comments).
- `Element lastChild(bool includeAllElements = false)`: Returns the last child.
- `auto children(VisitOrder order = VisitOrder.Normal)(bool returnAllElements = false)`: Immediate children range.
- `auto descendants(VisitOrder order = VisitOrder.Normal)(bool returnAllElements = false)`: Recursive descendants range.
- `bool contains(Element e, bool deep = true)`: Checks if the element contains another.

### `VisitOrder` (Enum)
Used with `children` and `descendants` ranges.
- `VisitOrder.Normal`: Forward traversal.
- `VisitOrder.Reverse`: Backward traversal.

### `Attribute` (Struct)
- `string name`: Attribute name.
- `string value`: Attribute value.

## Range Helpers
Since most search methods return lazy ranges, these helpers facilitate common operations:

- `frontOrThrow(range)`: Returns the first element or throws `Exception` if empty.
- `frontOr(range, fallback)`: Returns the first element or the `fallback` element.
- `frontOrInit(range)`: Returns the first element or an uninitialized `Element` (`null`).

## Fragment Handling
Use `.asFragment` on a string to indicate it should be parsed as HTML when using `appendChild`, `prependChild`, `appendSibling`, `prependSibling`, or `outerHTML`.

```d
import parserino;
auto doc = Document("<div></div>");
doc.body.firstChild.appendChild("<b>Bold</b>".asFragment);
// Result: <body><div><b>Bold</b></div></body>
```

## Practical Examples

### Wikipedia Scraper (Lazy Processing)
```d
import std, parserino;
void main() {
    auto data = "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:Random".get.to!string;
    Document doc = Document(data);

    // Get the canonical URL from <head>
    auto canonicalUrl = doc.head.bySelector("link[rel=canonical]").frontOrThrow.getAttribute("href");

    // Take the first 5 internal links lazily
    auto internalLinks = doc.byId("mw-content-text")
        .byTagName("a")
        .filter!(x => x.getAttribute("href").startsWith("/wiki/"))
        .filter!(x => !x.getAttribute("href").startsWith("/wiki/File:"))
        .take(5);

    foreach(l; internalLinks)
        writeln(l.getAttribute("title"), " => ", l.getAttribute("href"));
}
```

### DOM Manipulation
```d
import std.stdio, parserino;
void main() {
    Document doc = Document("<html><body><h1>Old Title</h1></body></html>");
    auto h1 = doc.byTagName("h1").frontOrThrow;
    h1.innerText = "New Title";

    auto p = doc.createElement("p");
    p.innerText = "A new paragraph.";
    p.setAttribute("class", "description");
    doc.body.appendChild(p);

    writeln(doc.toString());
}
```

## Notes on Performance
Parserino uses lazy ranges for traversal. This means searching through a large document often doesn't require visiting every node unless the entire range is consumed (e.g., using `.array` or `.take(N)`).
